other_observers: none; There was an eBird report that I did not see, and apparently two males and one female were originally present in this location. I followed the bird to most of its foraging spots and did not think there was any other PRAWs present on this date.
specific_location: Cold Spring Gulch in Corwina Park in Bear Creek Canyon.
description: A mostly yellow warbler with a long dark bill. Underneath all yellow with a few rows of blackish streaks on the flanks. Face with a dark eyeline and a dark loop attached to the dark lore and bending below the eye but not connected like a full adult would show. Crown, back, and nape a dull olive green. Back with a few parallel rows of dull reddish streaks (seen at close range).Behaviors: Singing and moving at mid and low elevations in a riparian area in Ponderosa pines but mostly in shrubby willows. Foraging between singing.Call: sang a loud rising series of zee-zee-zee-zee notes.Plumage: possibly 2nd yr
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
